chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Crl. Revision Application No.S-41 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A No.2021/2025. 2. For orders on office objection. 3. For orders on M.A No.2022/2025. 4. For hearing of main case. 24.10.2025 Mr. Arsalan Ali, advocate for the applicant. = 1 Urgency granted. 2 Deferred. 3 Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 4 Learned counsel for the applicant Hassan Akhtar submits that the applicant is the biological father of minor Muhammad Akhtar (aged about 03 years) and the respondent Mst. Nida @ Iqra, the mother of the minor, has approached the learned Sessions Judge, Umerkot by way of filing application under section 491 Cr.P.C and on the direction of learned Sessions Judge, Umerkot, the custody of minor was handed over to the mother. It has been observed by the learned Sessions Judge, Umerkot that urgency involved in ensuring immediate welfare and restoring a proper custodial arrangement, this Court, exercising its jurisdiction as loco parentis under Section 491, Cr.P.C., is obligated to ensure the welfare and protection of the child through an efficacious and speedy remedy. However, he further directed that parties may approach to the Guardian and Wards Court for regulating the custody of the minor. In view of above, no case for revision is made out and no illegality has been committed by the learned trial court while passing the impugned order, therefore, the instant Criminal Revision Application is dismissed in limine. However, applicant is at liberty to approach concerned Guardian and Wards Court in accordance with law.

In view of above, no case for revision is made out and no illegality has been committed by the learned trial court while passing the impugned order, therefore, the instant Criminal Revision Application is dismissed in limine. However, applicant is at liberty to approach concerned Guardian and Wards Court in accordance with law. JUDGE *Faisal*
